name: website-maintainer
description: Use this skill when working on the Repomix documentation website in `website/` directory, including VitePress configuration, multi-language content, or translation workflows.
capabilities: Identify the VitePress directory structure under website/client
Point to per-language config files and shared config shards
Describe steps to add a new language locale
List where to edit guides, navigation, and shared settings
State translation rules for source and localized content
author: yamadashy
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and metadata. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file access capabilities exist. Static findings are false positives triggered by markdown syntax and file path references. The backtick patterns are markdown code block delimiters, not shell execution. No cryptographic functions, network calls, or file operations are present.
valueStatement: Keeping multilingual documentation consistent across 14 languages is slow and error prone. This skill provides clear paths and rules to update VitePress content and configuration safely.
limitations: Does not generate new website content automatically
Does not validate builds or run VitePress commands
Scope is limited to the Repomix website documentation structure
Does not cover non-VitePress documentation systems

useCases.title: Plan a new locale
useCases.description: Follow the steps to add a new language with correct config, search, and content folders.

useCases.title: Update a guide
useCases.description: Find the right guide file and update localized content without changing code examples.

useCases.title: Adjust navigation
useCases.description: Edit sidebar and navigation labels in the proper language config file.

promptTemplates.title: Find guide location
promptTemplates.scenario: You need to edit a Japanese guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me where to edit the installation guide for Japanese and what file path to open.

promptTemplates.title: Add a locale
promptTemplates.scenario: You want to add a new language
promptTemplates.prompt: List the exact steps to add a new language locale to the VitePress site and which files to create or edit.

promptTemplates.title: Update nav labels
promptTemplates.scenario: You need sidebar changes for French
promptTemplates.prompt: Which config file controls sidebar labels for French, and where should I edit them?

promptTemplates.title: Check translation rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Summarize the translation rules and what must remain unchanged when localizing content.

bestPractices: Keep English as the source of truth for content changes
Update navigation and sidebar labels in the correct per-language config
Preserve code examples and CLI options exactly during translation
antiPatterns: Editing shared config shard when only one language needs changes
Modifying code samples or CLI flags while translating text
Adding a locale without updating search configuration in configShard

faq.question: Is this compatible with any VitePress site?
faq.answer: It targets the Repomix website structure and may not match other VitePress projects directly.

faq.question: Are there limits on how many languages I can add?
faq.answer: No explicit limits exist, but each new locale needs a config file and content folder.

faq.question: Can it integrate with external translation tools?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance only and does not integrate with translation services.

faq.question: Does this skill access or store my data?
faq.answer: It contains no executable code and does not access files, environment, or networks.

faq.question: What if the sidebar does not update after editing?
faq.answer: Verify you edited the correct per-language config file and that it is properly imported.

faq.question: How does it compare to a generic documentation guide?
faq.answer: It is specific to the Repomix VitePress layout, translation workflow, and 14-language structure.
